an9.64:1.3: Sensual desire as a hindrance, ill will as a hindrance, sloth & drowsiness as a hindrance, restlessness & anxiety as a hindrance, and uncertainty as a hindrance.
an9.64:2.1: “To abandon these five hindrances, one should develop the four establishings of mindfulness.
an9.64:2.3: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself…
an9.64:2.6: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
an9.64:2.7: To abandon the five hindrances, one should develop these four establishings of mindfulness.”
